name: Close window on focus loss
acceptanceCriteria: Window hides when user clicks on another application (both Windows and macOS)
Window hides when user Alt-Tabs / Cmd-Tabs away
Existing close behaviors still work (Escape key, selecting an item)
Daemon mode and standalone --gui mode both support focus-loss hiding
description: When the project-switch GUI window loses focus (user clicks away or switches to another app), automatically hide the window. This matches the expected behavior of launcher-style UIs like Spotlight/Alfred.

tasks.task: Add viewport().focused check in DaemonApp::update to call state.hide() when window loses focus
Add viewport().focused check in LauncherApp::update to call state.hide() when window loses focus
Test on macOS that clicking away, Cmd-Tabbing, and existing Escape/Enter behaviors all work correctly

name: UI window does not open URLs in browser when input has no match
acceptanceCriteria: When the user presses Enter with input that matches no items and is_url() returns true, the URL is opened in the default browser
Bare domains (e.g. staffordwilliams.com) are prefixed with https://
URLs already prefixed with http:// or https:// are opened as-is
description:
**Repro:**
1. Open the project-switch UI window (e.g. via hotkey)
2. Type a URL such as `staffordwilliams.com`
3. Press Enter when no items match

**Expected:** The URL is detected, prefixed with `https://` if needed, and opened in the default browser.

**Actual:** Nothing happens. The URL detection logic in `execute_action()` is not reached from the UI window code path.

name: Hotkey window appears but doesn't receive focus ~50% of the time
acceptanceCriteria: The project-switch window reliably receives focus when launched via the hotkey
The text field is focused and ready for input immediately after the window appears
The window dismisses normally on focus loss regardless of how it was activated
description:
**Repro:**
1. Have another application focused (e.g. a browser or editor)
2. Press ALT+SPACE to trigger the hotkey
3. The project-switch list window appears

**Expected:** The project-switch window appears on top AND receives input focus, with the text field ready for typing.

**Actual:** ~50% of the time, the window appears on top of other windows but does not receive focus. The previously focused application retains focus. Because the window never got focus, clicking elsewhere doesn't dismiss it — you must first click the project-switch window to focus it, then click away to dismiss it.

name: Graceful incomplete calculator expressions
acceptanceCriteria: Typing =5+ shows = 5+... (echoed partial expression) instead of a parse error
Typing =5*(3+ shows = 5*(3+... (incomplete due to unclosed paren or trailing op)
Typing =abc shows invalid expression
Typing =5+3 still shows = 8 as before
Both the egui UI and CLI autocomplete suggestions reflect the same behavior
description: Distinguish between incomplete and genuinely invalid calculator expressions. While typing an incomplete expression (e.g. =5+), show = 5+... instead of a raw parse error. Only show invalid expression for input that is truly wrong (e.g. =abc).

comments.text: Added is_incomplete_expr helper, CalcResult three-state enum (Ok/Incomplete/Invalid), and eval_calc_input wrapper. Updated all call sites (state.rs, window.rs, list.rs) to use the new classification. Incomplete expressions (trailing operator or unclosed parens) now return CalcResult::Incomplete; genuinely invalid input returns CalcResult::Invalid. Added 11 unit tests covering both detection and classification.

Rendered CalcResult::Incomplete as '= {expr}...' in egui (gray, 28pt) and CLI autocomplete (dimmed). Updated CLI submit path to show '= {expr}...' for incomplete and 'invalid expression' for genuinely invalid input. All three UIs now share consistent behavior via eval_calc_input.

Added normalize_decimals to handle bare decimal points (.01 -> 0.01), three-state CalcResult enum (Ok/Incomplete/Invalid), and is_incomplete_expr helper. Incomplete expressions show echoed input with ellipsis; genuinely invalid input shows 'invalid expression'. Both egui and CLI paths share eval_calc_input for consistent behavior.

tasks.task: Add an is_incomplete_expr helper that returns true when input ends with an operator or has unclosed parens
Update eval_calculator (or add a wrapper) to return a three-state result: Ok(value), Incomplete(partial), or Invalid
Add unit tests for incomplete vs invalid detection
Update window.rs to render incomplete state as = {expr}... in an appropriate style
Update list.rs autocomplete to show the same partial echo for incomplete expressions
Update the CLI submit path to show a friendlier message for incomplete expressions

name: Recent actions on list open
acceptanceCriteria: Executing any item from the list records it in a history file (max 10, most recent first, deduplicated)
When the list opens with empty input, only recent items are shown
Re-executing an item moves it to the top of the recents list
When the user types any character, filtering switches to the full item list (existing behavior)
If a recent item no longer exists in the config/shortcuts, it is excluded from the recents display
Works in both GUI and terminal modes
description: When the list UI opens, display the most recent actions (up to 10) instead of the full list. As soon as the user types, switch to filtering the full item list. Recent actions are persisted across sessions.

comments.text: Added history module (src/history.rs) that reads/writes ~/.project-switch-history.yml with load() and record() functions. record() deduplicates by moving re-executed items to top and caps at 10 entries. Integrated into execute_action() so both GUI and terminal modes record history. Includes 5 unit tests covering add, dedup, cap, re-execute, and missing-file scenarios.

Integrated recent history into UI. WindowState accepts recent_keys and filtered_items() returns validated recents when input is empty, full-list filtering when typing. Same logic applied to terminal/inquire ListAutocomplete. Daemon mode also loads history. Added 5 tests for recents behavior (show recents, filter full list on typing, exclude missing items, fallback when no/invalid recents).

Review: daemon mode was not refreshing recent_keys on window reopen — recents would be stale after first action. Fixed by adding set_recent_keys() to WindowState and calling it alongside set_items() in both hotkey and tray-menu handlers.

Recent actions on list open: history module records executed items (max 10, deduplicated, most recent first). GUI and terminal modes show recents on empty input, switch to full-list filtering on typing. Missing items excluded. Fixed daemon mode to refresh recents on each window open.

tasks.task: Add a history module that reads/writes a recent-actions file (e.g. ~/.project-switch-history.yml)
Record the item key on each execution in execute_action(), cap at 10 entries, deduplicate by moving re-executed items to the top
Pass recent history into WindowState. When input is empty, filtered_items() returns only recent items (validated against current items). When input is non-empty, use existing full-list filtering.
Apply same recent-items logic to the terminal/inquire mode in execute()

name: Half-second delay between hotkey and GUI list appearing
acceptanceCriteria: The GUI list appears with no perceptible delay after pressing ALT+SPACE
description:
**Repro:**
1. Have the hotkey service running
2. Press ALT+SPACE

**Expected:** The list GUI appears instantly.

**Actual:** There is a noticeable ~500ms delay before the window appears.

comments.text: Phase 1 changes: (1) hotkey service: changed taskkill from blocking .status() to non-blocking .spawn() — saves ~50-100ms. (2) GUI: split load_items into load_command_items (fast, config-only) and collect_shortcut_items (slow, filesystem scan). execute_gui now loads commands immediately, opens the window, and collects shortcuts on a background thread via mpsc channel. LauncherApp polls the channel each frame and appends shortcuts when ready. (3) Added WindowState::append_items to merge shortcuts without resetting selection/input.

Made taskkill non-blocking in hotkey service (~50-100ms saved). Deferred shortcut filesystem scanning to a background thread in GUI mode — window opens immediately with commands, shortcuts merge in asynchronously via mpsc channel.

Review: launch-first-kill-later pattern fixed the race where non-blocking taskkill killed the new process. Window now opens immediately — new process spawns first, gets foreground permission, then old instances are killed by PID exclusion via wmic.

Eliminated hotkey-to-GUI delay by deferring shortcut filesystem scanning to a background thread (window opens immediately with commands only, shortcuts merge in async) and reordering launch_project_switch to spawn the new process before killing old instances (PID-excluded wmic, non-blocking).

name: Record expressions and file paths in history
acceptanceCriteria: Evaluating a calculator expression (e.g. =5+3) records it in history
Opening a file path records it in history
Recent expressions appear formatted as =expr -> result in the recents list
Recent file paths appear with a [path] prefix in the recents list
Selecting a recent expression populates the input and shows the result
Selecting a recent file path populates the input and opens the path
Existing command/shortcut history behaviour is unchanged
description: Calculator expressions and file paths should be recorded in the history file alongside command/shortcut keys, so they appear in the recents list when the input is empty. Selecting a recent expression re-evaluates it; selecting a recent file path opens it.

comments.text: Record calc expressions and file paths in history. Added history::record() calls in: (1) execute_action() for file paths before launch, (2) execute() terminal path for successful calc expressions, (3) GUI window.rs calculator Enter handler. Added 6 unit tests covering expression recording, file path recording, deduplication, mixed entries, and cap enforcement.

Phase 2: Display and execute recents. Added FilteredEntry enum (Item/Expression/Path) and filtered_entries() to WindowState for mixed recents including non-item history. GUI Normal mode renders expressions as '=expr -> result' (green) and file paths as '[path] path' (blue). Selecting an expression populates input triggering calculator mode; selecting a path opens it. CLI matching_suggestions and get_completion updated similarly. Extracted open_path_and_hide helper to deduplicate. 8 new unit tests for mixed recents display, formatting, count, navigation, and selection.

Calculator expressions and file paths are recorded in history and appear in the recents list with distinct formatting (green =expr -> result, blue [path] prefix). Selecting a recent expression re-enters calculator mode; selecting a recent path opens it. Existing command/shortcut history unchanged.

tasks.task: Record calc expressions in history from both terminal and GUI execute paths
Record file paths in history from both terminal and GUI execute paths
Add unit tests for expression and file path recording
Extend filtered_items and recents rendering to handle non-item history entries with formatting (=5+3 -> 8 for expressions, [path] for file paths)
Wire selection of recent expressions to populate input and evaluate, and recent file paths to populate input and open
Add unit tests for mixed recents display and selection behaviour

name: List selection ignores arrow-key position and always opens first item
acceptanceCriteria: Pressing Enter after arrowing down opens the item currently highlighted, not the first item
description:
**Repro:**
1. Run `project-switch list`
2. Arrow down to any item other than the first
3. Press Enter

**Expected:** The highlighted/selected item opens.

**Actual:** The first item in the list opens regardless of selection.

comments.text: Root cause: execute_and_hide() always passed state.input (text field) to execute_action, ignoring the selected index. Fixed by extracting the highlighted item's key from filtered_entries[selected] and passing that instead.

Fixed execute_and_hide to use the selected item's key instead of the raw text input. When Enter is pressed, the highlighted entry's key is now extracted from filtered_entries[selected] and passed to execute_action.

Fixed list selection: Enter now opens the highlighted item instead of always opening the first item. Changed execute_and_hide to accept an explicit action_input parameter resolved from entries[selected].

name: Monitor selection for hotkey launcher
acceptanceCriteria: Tray context menu has a "Monitor" submenu listing connected monitors (Monitor 1, Monitor 2, etc.)
Selected monitor has a checkmark; defaults to Monitor 1
Selection persists to ~/.project-switch.yml across restarts
Selecting a different monitor immediately launches the window on that monitor
project-switch list --gui --monitor N positions the window centered on monitor N
description: Add a "Monitor" submenu to the hotkey system tray context menu that lists connected monitors. Selecting a monitor persists the choice and immediately shows the launcher window on that monitor. The selection is passed as a --monitor CLI arg to project-switch list --gui.

comments.text: Phase 1: Added Monitor submenu to hotkey tray menu. Enumerates monitors via tao, persists selection to ~/.project-switch.yml, updates checkmarks on selection, and passes --monitor N to project-switch list --gui. Extracted load_config_doc/save_config_doc helpers to eliminate duplication.

Added Monitor submenu to hotkey tray context menu: enumerates connected monitors via tao, builds CheckMenuItem submenu, persists selection to ~/.project-switch.yml, passes --monitor N arg to project-switch list --gui. Extracted shared config helpers to eliminate duplication.

Phase 2 complete: added --monitor N CLI arg to list subcommand, threaded through to launcher_options/execute_gui, and replaced centered:true with Win32 EnumDisplayMonitors-based positioning that centers the window on the Nth monitor (sorted left-to-right). Falls back to centered:true when no monitor specified or on non-Windows.

Added --monitor N CLI arg to list subcommand. Threads monitor value through execute_gui to launcher_options. On Windows, enumerates monitors via EnumDisplayMonitors/GetMonitorInfoW, sorts left-to-right, and centers the 700x500 window on the target monitor using with_position. Falls back to centered:true when --monitor is omitted or on non-Windows.

Added Monitor submenu to hotkey tray menu with persist-to-config selection. Passes --monitor N to project-switch list --gui which centers the window on the Nth monitor using DPI-aware Win32 EnumDisplayMonitors positioning. Fixed config parsing crash (deny_unknown_fields) and eliminated window flash via off-screen initial placement.

plan.name: Hotkey crate — monitor submenu and config

tasks.task: Add monitor index read/write to hotkey config.rs (persist to ~/.project-switch.yml)
Enumerate monitors via tao and build a Monitor submenu with CheckMenuItems
Handle menu events: update config, update checkmarks, and immediately launch project-switch on the selected monitor
Pass --monitor N arg in launch_project_switch

plan.name: Main binary — accept --monitor and position window

tasks.task: Add --monitor CLI arg to the list subcommand
Thread the monitor value through to launcher_options / execute_gui
Replace centered: true with calculated position to center on the target monitor

name: Surface config errors in GUI mode and log file
acceptanceCriteria: All errors written to eprintln! in main() are also appended to ~/.project-switch.log with a timestamp
When --gui flag is active and an error occurs, a native MessageBoxW dialog displays the error instead of printing to stderr
Log file doesn't grow unbounded — truncate if it exceeds 64KB
description: When project-switch list --gui fails (e.g. bad config), the error is printed to stderr which is invisible because the hotkey daemon spawns the process with CREATE_NO_WINDOW. Add a log file that always captures errors, and show a native message box when in --gui mode.

comments.text: Added log::append_error() in src/utils/log.rs — writes timestamped error lines to ~/.project-switch.log with 64KB truncation. Called from main() error handler for all error paths.
Phase 2: GUI errors now show a native MessageBoxW dialog on Windows when --gui is active. Non-Windows falls back to eprintln. Press Enter prompt is skipped in GUI mode.
Added MessageBoxW error dialog for --gui mode on Windows with eprintln fallback on other platforms. Removed Press Enter prompt in GUI mode.
Added 'Open config file in editor?' Yes/No button to GUI error dialog. Clicking Yes opens ~/.project-switch.yml in VS Code via cmd /c code.
Errors logged to ~/.project-switch.log with timestamps (64KB cap). GUI mode shows MessageBoxW with Yes/No to open config in VS Code. Non-GUI mode prints to stderr with Press Enter prompt.

tasks.task: Add a function that appends a timestamped error line to ~/.project-switch.log, truncating the file if it exceeds 64KB
Call the log function from the main() error handler for all error paths
When --gui is active, show errors via MessageBoxW (Win32) instead of eprintln + Press Enter prompt. On non-Windows, fall back to eprintln.
Remove the Press Enter to exit prompt when in --gui mode

name: GUI launcher drops user args for URL commands
acceptanceCriteria: When a user types <key> <args> in the GUI launcher and presses Enter, the args are appended to the configured URL
Regression tests confirm args are preserved through the GUI code path for URL commands
description:
**Repro:**
1. Configure a URL command (e.g. `g` → `https://google.com/search?q=`)
2. Open the GUI launcher (ALT+SPACE)
3. Type `g some text` and press Enter

**Expected:** Browser opens `https://google.com/search?q=some%20text`

**Actual:** Browser opens `https://google.com/search?q=` with no query — `execute_and_hide` passes only the selected item's key to `execute_action()`, discarding the user's args.

comments.text: Fix: window.rs execute_and_hide now receives state.input (full user text) instead of item.key (just the matched key). Added regression tests in state.rs and launcher/mod.rs.

Fixed GUI launcher dropping user args for URL commands. Changed execute_and_hide call in window.rs to pass full state.input instead of just item.key. Added regression tests in state.rs and launcher/mod.rs confirming args are preserved through the GUI code path.

GUI launcher now passes full user input (key + args) to execute_action instead of just the selected item key. filter_items uses exact key match when input contains a space. Regression tests cover args preservation through filtering, state, resolution, and URL encoding.

name: Regression: list selection ignores arrow-key position (re-open #8)
acceptanceCriteria: Pressing Enter after arrowing down opens the item currently highlighted, not the first item
Add a test covering list selection to prevent future regressions
description:
**Repro:**
1. Run `project-switch list` with at least two items
2. Arrow down to the second item
3. Press Enter

**Expected:** The second (highlighted) item opens.

**Actual:** The first item opens regardless of selection.

comments.text: Fix: GUI Enter key now uses selected_action_input() to resolve the highlighted entry's key, instead of passing raw input text to resolve_item. Added 6 regression tests to state.rs covering navigation selection, args preservation, partial filters, empty lists, and recents.

Added selected_action_input() method to WindowState that resolves the highlighted entry key (preserving user args when applicable). Fixed window.rs Enter handler to use it instead of raw input text. Added 6 regression tests.
Fixed: selected_action_input uses highlighted entry index, not first item. Added 6 regression tests covering navigation, args, partial filter, empty list, and recents.

name: Nested projects under clients
acceptanceCriteria: ~/.project-switch.yml uses 'clients:' at the top level; nested 'projects:' under each client (0 or more)
A nested project has the same shape as a client (name, path, description, browser, commands) but cannot itself contain a 'projects' field
project-switch switch presents clients first; if the selected client has nested projects, a second prompt lets the user pick the client directly or one of its projects
Selecting a client with no projects (or choosing the client itself) uses only client-level + global commands
Selecting a nested project exposes: project commands + client commands (project overrides client on duplicate keys) + global commands (overridden by both)
Current selection persists across both levels via currentClient and currentProject keys in YAML
project-switch list and project-switch current reflect the selected client/project
The add subcommand is removed from the CLI
Old-schema config files (top-level 'projects:', 'currentProject:') are detected on load and rewritten in place to the new schema; included configs are migrated too
All existing config merging / include behaviour continues to work with the new schema
description: Rename the top-level 'projects' config key to 'clients' and allow each client to contain its own nested 'projects' array. A project inherits the client's commands, with project-level commands overriding on key collision. The 'switch' command becomes a two-step flow (pick client, then optionally pick a nested project). Old-schema config files are migrated in place on load.

comments.text: Renamed Project struct to Client, YAML keys projects/currentProject to clients/currentClient throughout code and examples. Added in-place schema migration for local and included configs with one-line notice. Updated call sites in switch, list, current, open, daemon; removed the add subcommand and src/commands/add.rs. Refreshed README and example configs.

Phase 2: added Project struct with deny_unknown_fields (prevents deeper nesting); Client.projects optional. Merge logic shared between client/project via merge_shared_fields! macro. Added currentProject YAML key and resolve_current() returning (client, Option<project>). Command precedence: project > client > global. switch.rs shows second Select with '<client> (client)' + nested projects when present. list/current/daemon show 'client / project' label; list uses scope_browser (project.browser || client.browser) fallback. Duplicate project names within a client fail validation.

Nested projects: added Project struct and optional Client.projects field; merge logic extended via shared-fields macro; currentProject YAML key and resolve_current() helper; switch.rs two-step flow (client then optional project); command resolution project>client>global; list/current/daemon reflect selected project; example-config.yml, example-include-config.yml and README updated.

plan.name: Rename projects to clients, migrate old configs, remove add

tasks.task: Rename Project struct to Client in config.rs; rename YAML key 'projects' to 'clients'; rename current_project / currentProject to current_client / currentClient

On config load, detect old schema (top-level 'projects:' and/or 'currentProject:') and rewrite the file in place (both the local config and any included config); rename keys, preserve other fields; log a one-line notice when migration runs
Update all call sites (switch, list, current, launcher, history, hotkey) to use the new names
Remove the add subcommand (commands/add.rs, its mod.rs entry, and its clap wiring)
Update README examples to use 'clients:'

plan.name: Nested projects with two-step switch and command inheritance

tasks.task: Add a Project struct (same fields as Client minus nested projects) and optional projects: Vec<Project> field on Client; validate against deeper nesting and duplicate project names within a client
Extend config merging to merge nested projects arrays by name (same rules as client merging)
Add currentProject YAML key alongside currentClient; update resolve_current_* helpers to return the resolved client + optional project
Update switch.rs: after picking a client, if it has nested projects, show a second Select with '<client-name> (client)' plus each nested project; persist both keys
Update command resolution: project -> client -> global when a project is active; client -> global when only a client is active
Update list and current commands to show the selected nested project and effective command set
Update launcher / hotkey paths that read the current selection so hotkey-launched list shows the correct effective commands
